scope: 92 Figma runs reported in rubric comparison
interpretation: Observable expert workflow anchors improved cross-LLM judge agreement relative to generic rubrics
caveat: Comparison changed author source rubric count grouping weights anchor language and observability together

interpretation: Generic rubrics had lower reported inter-judge agreement
caveat: Does not isolate expert authorship as the cause

interpretation: Expert workflow anchors reduced reported judge variance
Generic rubrics had higher reported judge variance
Fine-grained automated output grades weakly matched human judgments
caveat: One Figma domain expert limits human-baseline generality

interpretation: Fine-grained automated skill grades weakly matched human judgments
caveat: No independent human process annotation validates the skill judges

scope: Claude Codex and Gemini harness/model systems
interpretation: Skill effects were directional and heterogeneous
caveat: Sample is underpowered and harness/model/integration factors remain bundled

scope: Subset of the 7 paired Figma runs
interpretation: Large reported directional gain for one configured system
caveat: Too few runs for a stable causal adoption claim

interpretation: Public procedure may improve completion for some systems
caveat: Deployment difference alone cannot separate procedure content from integration and stochasticity

reported_value: 60 percent of pairwise-winning Figma outputs met expert >=4 threshold
scope: 31 rated Figma tasks with one domain expert
interpretation: Relative preference does not imply absolute readiness
caveat: Single-expert threshold is not a universal professional standard
